Name: EVOL
Notes: (February 5, 2016) (EVOL is released)
Leak Date:
In August, 2014, Future announced plans for EVOL, which is pronounced “evil” and is “love” spelled backwards. The songs are not about falling out of love, but rather about the unexpected forms love can take.

Released on February 5th, 2016, EVOL is Future’s fourth studio album. After a phenomenal 2015 run, consisting of Beast Mode, 56 Nights, DS2 and WATTBA with Drake, EVOL continues King Future’s impressive streak.

The album was premiered by the special-cloth-loving DJ Khaled on his first show on Apple Music’s Beats 1 Radio.

Name: Future
Notes: (Februay 17, 2017) (Future releases)
Leak Date:
FUTURE is the fifth album by Atlanta rapper Future. It was released on February 17, 2017. The self-titled album was announced by Future through social media on February 14, 2017, along with the option to pre-order and tour dates for the Nobody Safe Tour.

The original release of FUTURE contained 17 featureless tracks that spanned just over an hour.

In June of 2017, Future added “Used To This”, the official “Mask Off” Remix, and “Extra Luv” to the album’s tracklist.

Name:
Super Slimey
(Collab with Young Thug)
Notes: (10/20/2017) "SUPER SLIMEY" is officially released
Leak Date: "SUPER SLIMEY" is a collaborative mixtape by Future and Young Thug, released on October 20, 2017. The project features the two Atlanta rappers flowing over trap production by 808 Mafia, Wheezy, London and more. There is only one feature on this project, and that’s from Offset, a member of the Migos group. The project began around September 2016 after the two squashed their beef in at Future's birthday party. After taking time away to finish their own albums, they recorded further in 2017 and finished up the project a bit more than a year after it began.

Name:
20 Hoes
(with Young Thug)
Notes: “20 Hoes” is a leaked collaboration between Future & Young Thug. While this version of the song seems unfinished, a remix to it featuring Drake was teased via Snapchat in March 2018, then again in May 2020. It’s likely that this was the original version before Drake was added to the song, but it should be noted that Thug’s ad libs were not present in the March 2018 snippet with Drake, while they were present in the May 2020 snippet. However, it seems Thug and Drake did not record the song together, as Thug’s ad libs over Drake’s verse in the other version are taken from Thug’s outro in this version. This version of the song leaked online on July 25, 2019.

Notes: (July 6, 2018) (Beast Mode 2 Is released)
Leak Date: Beast Mode 2 is a mixtape by American rapper Future and American record producer Zaytoven. It was released on July 6, 2018, by Freebandz and Epic Records for streaming and digital download, produced in its entirety by Zaytoven. It is the sequel to Future and Zaytoven's 2015 mixtape Beast Mode, and succeeds the release of the 2017 studio albums Future and Hndrxx, as well as collaborative mixtape Super Slimey with Young Thug and the soundtrack to 2018 film Superfly. The mixtape consists of nine tracks and includes a guest appearance by Young Scooter.

Leak Date: Future & Juice WRLD Present… WRLD ON DRUGS, or simply WRLD ON DRUGS, is a collaborative album between Future and Juice WRLD. Future has three solo songs on the album, while Juice WRLD only has one. The album’s lead single, “Fine China,” was released on October 15, 2018. The album boasts features from Lil Wayne, Young Thug, Gunna, Nicki Minaj, Yung Bans, and Young Scooter. The album is primarily produced by Wheezy, who Future regularly works with. The album also features production from Murda Beatz, Oogie Mane, CuBeatz, SinGrinch, Psymun, Richie Souf, and more. The intro and outro tracks were mixed by Serban Ghenea, while all the rest were mixed by Alex Tumay

Name: THE WIZRD
Notes: (Jan. 18, 2019) (THE WIZRD IS RELEASED)
Leak Date:
Future Hndrxx Presents: The WIZRD, or simply The WIZRD, is Future’s seventh solo studio album. The album boasts features from Travis Scott, Gunna and Young Thug.
The album’s lead single, “Crushed Up,” was released on January 4, 2019. Later that day, Epic Records announced the album’s release date but did not give a title.
On January 8, 2019, Future released the album’s second single, “Jumpin on a Jet.” He also revealed the album’s title and artwork.
A week before the album’s release, THE WIZRD documentary was released exclusively on Apple Music.
On January 14, 2019, Future revealed the album’s official tracklist.

SAVE ME
Notes: (June 7, 2019) (Save Me is released)
Leak Date:
SAVE ME is Future’s debut EP and his second release of 2019, following January’s The WIZRD. The seven-track project shows a more versatile and vulnerable side of Future than previous works.

Future began teasing the project on May 23, 2019, when he purged his Instagram and posted a clip of “Love Thy Enemies.” This led fans to believe that a new project was incoming.

On June 1, 2019, Future posted a clip of the music video for “Government Official.” He followed this two days later with a clip of “XanaX Damage.”

On June 5, 2019, Future revealed the title, release date, and artwork for the project. He also set the location of the post as “Oh Fuck, I Have Made A Huge Mistake.” Hours later, Apple Music confirmed that it was an EP.

HIGH OFF LIFE
(Life Is Good)
Notes: (May 15, 2020) (High Off Life is released)
Leak Date: On July 12, 2019, Future released the album’s lead single, “100 Shooters.” Eleven days later, Future released another single, “Undefeated.” Its cover used a similar aesthetic to “Shooters,” but it was only a promotional single. The second single, “Last Name,” was released on November 15. On January 10, 2020, Future released the record’s third single, “Life Is Good,” which was a notable reunion with Drake. After a successful debut, the song received an official remix a month later, adding Lil Baby and DaBaby to the track. The remix serves as the fourth single.The fifth and final pre-release single, “Tycoon,” was released on April 1.On April 7, 2020, XXL published their interview with Future for their Spring 2020 issue. Future initially stated that Life Is Good was the album’s title. He also described its creative process and what the album meant to him. On May 12, 2020, DJ ESCO, the album’s executive producer, officially announced the LP by posting its tracklist on his Instagram. This revealed that the album’s title had been changed to High Off Life. Hours later, Future confirmed the album via a tweet, and he also posted the official artwork

The project features production from some more producers such as DJ ESCO, D. Hill, London on da Track and more.  After Uzi recorded solo songs with no paticular direction in early 2020, they would release two singles together, "Over Your Head" and "Patek", which coincidentally was on Uzi's birthday. They would later release the trailer for the album (which used the scrapped intro, "Astronauts"), revealing the release date and so on from there. The album was released unexpectedly to some, with somewhat of a positive reception. Uzi would tease the deluxe, which ended up dropping just a week later.

Name: I NEVER  LIKED YOU
Notes: (April 29, 2022) (I NEVER LIKED YOU Releases)
Leak Date:
I NEVER LIKED YOU is Future’s ninth studio album, the follow-up to his May 2020 album, High Off Life.

Speculations began during an Instagram Story in January 2022, in which Future is seen working in their first join since 2019 with his most frequent collaborator, Metro Boomin, quoting:

back 2 the basics @future

On February 11, Future released his first 2022 single, “Worst Day,” for Valentine’s Day, making it the first promotional single for the album.

On April 4, Future hyped his fans with a tweet, saying that his frequent collaborator and Freebandz member, Young Scooter told him that he should release his next album on the same month — Later on the same day, he posted another tweet about the album, saying that new music would be coming very soon.

The next day during an Instagram Story, Scooter teased a short-clip of a new Kanye West collaboration with Future, and later, he responds on his own Instagram Story, confirming that the collaboration will be on his album, saying:

Ima have album date this week jus holdup .. YE SPAZZED ON DIS SHIT.

On April 7 and 8, Future took Twitter to confirm that Babyface Ray and FKA twigs would also be featured on his new album, alongside that he would announce the date and name of the album soon in another tweet.

On April 13, Future would post on Instagram that his new album would be coming out on April 29, 2022, with the title still not released to the public.

On April 25, 2022, Future announced in Instagram the title and album cover of the album — The cover is part of Future’s photo shoot for an interview with GQ on April 19.

The next day, Future confirms “KEEP IT BURNIN” with Ye in a short clip of the video on Instagram, that would appear on the album — the song was first seen during the official Tracklist for Kanye West’s album, Donda 2, and an unfinished version of the song appeared on Ye’s Stem Player, later was removed from the song list.

Future revealed on Instagram the official tracklist of the album on April 27, with features of Gunna, Young Thug, Drake, Tems, Kanye West, EST Gee and Kodak Black.

Name:
🥉TRIM [V1] 
(feat. Playboi Carti & Travis Scott) (prod. TM88, Akachi, DJ Moon, Sonickaboom, C$D Sid & Macnificent) 
(Really I'm Trim)
Notes: A video of Carti listening to a song with DJ Swamp Izzo tag and Future feature while arriving late for Clout festival surfaced on Jul 17, 2024. Partial LQ surfaced July 18th, 2024. Allegedly scrapped from Future's next album Mixtape Pluto. Song was offered to Hells who then stalled and ended up leaving. Billboards spotted saying 'TRIM' in LA, possibly relating to the song. Later released on MUSIC with a new Carti verse, a bunch of Swamp Izzo tags, and awful mixing. It's also been confirmed that this former version has a Travis Scott verse (yet to be heard) that got scrapped for the official release.

As a whole, these three projects form a contrast, with Future returning from the mainstream spotlight to his mixtape origins.

Name:
⭐Snort U A Line
(Feat. Young Thug)
(Prod. London on da Track, Dez Wright & 9Jay)
(Smoke A Joint)
Notes:
“Snort U A Line” is a completely reconstructed version of an unreleased Young Thug and Veedoe collaboration called “Smoke A Joint” which was recorded in 2019. The song was updated sometime in 2023 or 2024 and changed to a completely different instrumental from London on da track and a feature from Future. The original version of the song was produced by Pi'erre Bourne.

This version leaked on October 8, 2024.

Name:
⭐Ready To Slide
(Prod. Southside, Wheezy, & Smatt Sertified)
Notes: “Ready To Slide” is an Mixtape Pluto era song that was recorded on February 16, 2024. A low quality snippet of the song surfaced in August 2025. The song leaked on October 25, 2025. As the leak was starting to get popular, Future immediately filmed a music video for it and teased it on November 6, 2025.

Name:
✨Young Thug - Pardon My Back
(Feat. Future, Lil Baby)
(Prod. Wheezy & Juke Wong)
Notes:
This is the original version of “Pardon My Back” which was recorded sometime in early 2025. The song was first previewed on May 5, 2025. Lil baby previewed the song again in a promotional video for his tour on May 25, 2025. The song leaked on August 18, 2025.

This version includes a verse from future with Wheezy production on his verse. It also has a different outro. All of these aspects of the song were absent in the official release of the song.

Name:
✨Getting Money
(Feat. Young Thug)
Notes:
“Getting Money” is an unreleased collaboration between Young Thug and Future that was recorded sometime in early 2025. The song was unheard until a low quality snippet surfaced in summer 2025.

The song leaked on October 25, 2025.
